verses.latin_vulgate: Simile est regnum cælorum homini patrifamilias, qui exiit primo mane conducere operarios in vineam suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁμοία γάρ ἐστιν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν ἀνθρώπῳ οἰκοδεσπότῃ ὅστις ἐξῆλθεν ἅμα πρωῒ μισθώσασθαι ἐργάτας εἰς τὸν ἀμπελῶνα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man *that is* an householder, which went out early in the morning to hire labourers into his vineyard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conventione autem facta cum operariis ex denario diurno, misit eos in vineam suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συμφωνήσας δὲ μετὰ τῶν ἐργατῶν ἐκ δηναρίου τὴν ἡμέραν ἀπέστειλεν αὐτοὺς εἰς τὸν ἀμπελῶνα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And when he had agreed with the labourers for a penny a day, he sent them into his vineyard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et egressus circa horam tertiam, vidit alios stantes in foro otiosos,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξελθὼν περὶ τὴν τρίτην ὥραν εἶδεν ἄλλους ἑστῶτας ἐν τῇ ἀγορᾷ ἀργούς
verses.king_james: And he went out about the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the marketplace,

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit illis: Ite et vos in vineam meam, et quod justum fuerit dabo vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀκείνοις εἶπεν ὑπάγετε καὶ ὑμεῖς εἰς τὸν ἀμπελῶνα καὶ ὃ ἐὰν ᾖ δίκαιον δώσω ὑμῖν οἵ δέ ἀπῆλθόν
verses.king_james: And said unto them; Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you. And they went their way.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illi autem abierunt. Iterum autem exiit circa sextam et nonam horam: et fecit similiter.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν ἐξελθὼν περὶ ἕκτην καὶ ἐννάτην ὥραν ἐποίησεν ὡσαύτως
verses.king_james: Again he went out about the sixth and ninth hour, and did likewise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Circa undecimam vero exiit, et invenit alios stantes, et dicit illis: Quid hic statis tota die otiosi?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ δὲ τὴν ἑνδεκάτην ὥραν ἐξελθὼν εὗρεν ἄλλους ἑστῶτας ἀργούς καὶ λέγει αὖτοῖς τί ὧδε ἑστήκατε ὅλην τὴν ἡμέραν ἀργοί
verses.king_james: And about the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle, and saith unto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicunt ei: Quia nemo nos conduxit. Dicit illis: Ite et vos in vineam meam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγουσιν αὐτῷ ὅτι οὐδεὶς ἡμᾶς ἐμισθώσατο λέγει αὐτοῖς ὑπάγετε καὶ ὑμεῖς εἰς τὸν ἀμπελῶνα καὶ ὁ ἐὰν ᾖ δίκαιον λήψεσθε
verses.king_james: They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard; and whatsoever is right, *that* shall ye receive.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum sero autem factum esset, dicit dominus vineæ procuratori suo: Voca operarios, et redde illis mercedem incipiens a novissimis usque ad primos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης λέγει ὁ κύριος τοῦ ἀμπελῶνος τῷ ἐπιτρόπῳ αὐτοῦ κάλεσον τοὺς ἐργάτας καὶ ἀπόδος αὐτοῖς τὸν μισθὸν ἀρξάμενος ἀπὸ τῶν ἐσχάτων ἕως τῶν πρώτων
verses.king_james: So when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the labourers, and give them *their* hire, beginning from the last unto the first.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum venissent ergo qui circa undecimam horam venerant, acceperunt singulos denarios.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλθόντες οἱ περὶ τὴν ἑνδεκάτην ὥραν ἔλαβον ἀνὰ δηνάριον
verses.king_james: And when they came that *were hired* about the eleventh hour, they received every man a penny.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venientes autem et primi, arbitrati sunt quod plus essent accepturi: acceperunt autem et ipsi singulos denarios.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλθόντες δὲ οἱ πρῶτοι ἐνόμισαν ὅτι πλεῖονα λήψονται καὶ ἔλαβον καὶ αὐτοί ἀνὰ δηνάριον
verses.king_james: But when the first came, they supposed that they should have received more; and they likewise received every man a penny.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accipientes murmurabant adversus patremfamilias,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λαβόντες δὲ ἐγόγγυζον κατὰ τοῦ οἰκοδεσπότου
verses.king_james: And when they had received *it*, they murmured against the goodman of the house,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes: Hi novissimi una hora fecerunt, et pares illos nobis fecisti, qui portavimus pondus diei, et æstus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντες ὅτι οὗτοι οἱ ἔσχατοι μίαν ὥραν ἐποίησαν καὶ ἴσους ἡμῖν αὐτοὺς ἐποίησας τοῖς βαστάσασιν τὸ βάρος τῆς ἡμέρας καὶ τὸν καύσωνα
verses.king_james: Saying, These last have wrought *but* one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us, which have borne the burden and heat of the day.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille respondens uni eorum, dixit: Amice, non facio tibi injuriam: nonne ex denario convenisti mecum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν ἑνὶ αὐτῶν ἑταῖρε οὐκ ἀδικῶ σε οὐχὶ δηναρίου συνεφώνησάς μοι
verses.king_james: But he answered one of them, and said, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst not thou agree with me for a penny?

verses.latin_vulgate: Tolle quod tuum est, et vade: volo autem et huic novissimo dare sicut et tibi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἆρον τὸ σὸν καὶ ὕπαγε θέλω δὲ τούτῳ τῷ ἐσχάτῳ δοῦναι ὡς καὶ σοί
verses.king_james: Take *that* thine *is*, and go thy way: I will give unto this last, even as unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aut non licet mihi quod volo, facere? an oculus tuus nequam est, quia ego bonus sum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ οὐκ ἔξεστίν μοι ποιῆσαι ὃ θέλω ἐν τοῖς ἐμοῖς εἰ ὁ ὀφθαλμός σου πονηρός ἐστιν ὅτι ἐγὼ ἀγαθός εἰμι
verses.king_james: Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? Is thine eye evil, because I am good?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic erunt novissimi primi, et primi novissimi. Multi enim sunt vocati, pauci vero electi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως ἔσονται οἱ ἔσχατοι πρῶτοι καὶ οἱ πρῶτοι ἔσχατοι πολλοὶ γὰρ εἰσιν κλητοί ὀλίγοι δὲ ἐκλεκτοί
verses.king_james: So the last shall be first, and the first last: for many be called, but few chosen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ascendens Jesus Jerosolymam, assumpsit duodecim discipulos secreto, et ait illis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναβαίνων ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα παρέλαβεν τοὺς δώδεκα μαθητὰς κατ᾽ ἰδίαν ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And Jesus going up to Jerusalem took the twelve disciples apart in the way, and said unto them,

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ascendimus Jerosolymam, et Filius hominis tradetur principibus sacerdotum, et scribis, et condemnabunt eum morte,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδού ἀναβαίνομεν εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα καὶ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου παραδοθήσεται τοῖς ἀρχιερεῦσιν καὶ γραμματεῦσιν καὶ κατακρινοῦσιν αὐτὸν θανάτῳ
verses.king_james: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the scribes, and they shall condemn him to death,

verses.latin_vulgate: et tradent eum gentibus ad illudendum, et flagellandum, et crucifigendum, et tertia die resurget.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παραδώσουσιν αὐτὸν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν εἰς τὸ ἐμπαῖξαι καὶ μαστιγῶσαι καὶ σταυρῶσαι καὶ τῇ τρίτῃ ἡμέρᾳ ἀναστήσεται
verses.king_james: And shall deliver him to the Gentiles to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify *him*: and the third day he shall rise again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc accessit ad eum mater filiorum Zebedæi cum filiis suis, adorans et petens aliquid ab eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε προσῆλθεν αὐτῷ ἡ μήτηρ τῶν υἱῶν ζεβεδαίου μετὰ τῶν υἱῶν αὐτῆς προσκυνοῦσα καὶ αἰτοῦσά τι παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Then came to him the mother of Zebedee's children with her sons, worshipping *him*, and desiring a certain thing of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixit ei: Quid vis? Ait illi: Dic ut sedeant hi duo filii mei, unus ad dexteram tuam, et unus ad sinistram in regno tuo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτῇ τί θέλεις λέγει αὐτῷ εἰπὲ ἵνα καθίσωσιν οὗτοι οἱ δύο υἱοί μου εἷς ἐκ δεξιῶν σου καὶ εἷς ἐξ εὐωνύμων ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ σου
verses.king_james: And he said unto her, What wilt thou? She saith unto him, Grant that these my two sons may sit, the one on thy right hand, and the other on the left, in thy kingdom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Jesus, dixit: Nescitis quid petatis. Potestis bibere calicem, quem ego bibiturus sum? Dicunt ei: Possumus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν οὐκ οἴδατε τί αἰτεῖσθε δύνασθε πιεῖν τὸ ποτήριον ὃ ἐγὼ μέλλω πίνειν καὶ τὸ βάπτισμα ὁ ἐγὼ βαπτίζομαι βαπτισθῆναι λέγουσιν αὐτῷ δυνάμεθα
verses.king_james: But Jesus answered and said, Ye know not what ye ask. Are ye able to drink of the cup that I shall drink of, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with? They say unto him, We are able.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illis: Calicem quidem meum bibetis: sedere autem ad dexteram meam vel sinistram non est meum dare vobis, sed quibus paratum est a Patre meo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς τὸ μὲν ποτήριόν μου πίεσθε καὶ τὸ βάπτισμα ὃ ἐγὼ βαπτίζομαι βαπτισθήσεσθε τὸ δὲ καθίσαι ἐκ δεξιῶν μου καὶ ἐξ εὐωνύμων μου οὐκ ἔστιν ἐμὸν δοῦναι ἀλλ᾽ οἷς ἡτοίμασται ὑπὸ τοῦ πατρός μου
verses.king_james: And he saith unto them, Ye shall drink indeed of my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with: but to sit on my right hand, and on my left, is not mine to give, but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared of my Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et audientes decem, indignati sunt de duobus fratribus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀκούσαντες οἱ δέκα ἠγανάκτησαν περὶ τῶν δύο ἀδελφῶν
verses.king_james: And when the ten heard *it*, they were moved with indignation against the two brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem vocavit eos ad se, et ait: Scitis quia principes gentium dominantur eorum: et qui majores sunt, potestatem exercent in eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς προσκαλεσάμενος αὐτοὺς εἶπεν οἴδατε ὅτι οἱ ἄρχοντες τῶν ἐθνῶν κατακυριεύουσιν αὐτῶν καὶ οἱ μεγάλοι κατεξουσιάζουσιν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: But Jesus called them *unto him*, and said, Ye know that the princes of the Gentiles exercise dominion over them, and they that are great exercise authority upon them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non ita erit inter vos: sed quicumque voluerit inter vos major fieri, sit vester minister:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχ οὕτως δέ ἔσται ἐν ὑμῖν ἀλλ᾽ ὃς ἐὰν θέλῃ ἐν ὑμῖν μέγας γενέσθαι ἔστω ὑμῶν διάκονος
verses.king_james: But it shall not be so among you: but whosoever will be great among you, let him be your minister;

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui voluerit inter vos primus esse, erit vester servus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὃς ἐὰν θέλῃ ἐν ὑμῖν εἶναι πρῶτος ἔστω ὑμῶν δοῦλος
verses.king_james: And whosoever will be chief among you, let him be your servant:

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut Filius hominis non venit ministrari, sed ministrare, et dare animam suam redemptionem pro multis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥσπερ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου οὐκ ἦλθεν διακονηθῆναι ἀλλὰ διακονῆσαι καὶ δοῦναι τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ λύτρον ἀντὶ πολλῶν
verses.king_james: Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et egredientibus illis ab Jericho, secuta est eum turba multa,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκπορευομένων αὐτῶν ἀπὸ ἰεριχὼ ἠκολούθησεν αὐτῷ ὄχλος πολύς
verses.king_james: And as they departed from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ecce duo cæci sedentes secus viam audierunt quia Jesus transiret: et clamaverunt, dicentes: Domine, miserere nostri, fili David.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού δύο τυφλοὶ καθήμενοι παρὰ τὴν ὁδόν ἀκούσαντες ὅτι ἰησοῦς παράγει ἔκραξαν λέγοντες ἐλέησον ἡμᾶς κύριε υἱὸς δαβίδ
verses.king_james: And, behold, two blind men sitting by the way side, when they heard that Jesus passed by, cried out, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, *thou* Son of David.

verses.latin_vulgate: Turba autem increpabat eos ut tacerent. At illi magis clamabant, dicentes: Domine, miserere nostri, fili David.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ὄχλος ἐπετίμησεν αὐτοῖς ἵνα σιωπήσωσιν οἱ δὲ μεῖζον ἔκραζον λέγοντες ἐλέησον ἡμᾶς κύριε υἱὸς δαβίδ
verses.king_james: And the multitude rebuked them, because they should hold their peace: but they cried the more, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, *thou* Son of David.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et stetit Jesus, et vocavit eos, et ait: Quid vultis ut faciam vobis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ στὰς ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐφώνησεν αὐτοὺς καὶ εἶπεν τί θέλετε ποιήσω ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: And Jesus stood still, and called them, and said, What will ye that I shall do unto you?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicunt illi: Domine, ut aperiantur oculi nostri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγουσιν αὐτῷ κύριε ἵνα ἀνοιχθῶσιν ἡμῶν οἱ ὀφθαλμοί
verses.king_james: They say unto him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misertus autem eorum Jesus, tetigit oculos eorum. Et confestim viderunt, et secuti sunt eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σπλαγχνισθεὶς δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἥψατο τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν αὐτῶν καὶ εὐθέως ἀνέβλεψαν αὐτῶν οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ καὶ ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: So Jesus had compassion *on them*, and touched their eyes: and immediately their eyes received sight, and they followed him.
